reasons: H_Mech factorial pilot shows encoder effect (0.519) is 65x larger than decoder effect (0.008), p=4.6e-126
Multi-seed validation confirms trained SAE absorption (0.355 +/- 0.026) >> random baseline (0.033 +/- 0.011), p=1.4e-08
H_Safe negative result (p=0.326) shows absorption is general geometric property, not safety-specific -- sharpens contribution
H3 steering negative result (ratio=0.97x, p=0.936) shows absorbed features are not more steerable -- rules out overclaim
Hierarchy strength ablation shows monotonic increase (0.416 -> 0.501 -> 0.544), ANOVA p=0.0 -- supports theoretical prediction
All experiments completed efficiently (~1 min each) -- excellent resource efficiency
concerns: L0 sparsity ablation shows opposite direction from prediction: lower sparsity -> higher absorption. Needs interpretation.
Held-out generalization shows r=1.0 correlation -- suspiciously perfect, may indicate train/test dependence.
Only one candidate (cand_p1) was tested in pilot. No backup candidate validated.
next_actions: Interpret L0 sparsity ablation counter-intuitive result -- investigate competitive dynamics explanation
Address held-out generalization concern -- verify train/test independence or redesign test
Synthesize results and proceed to paper writing with core story: encoder-driven absorption
Generate publication-ready figures for factorial design, multi-seed validation, and ablations
Frame negative results (H_Safe, H3) as scientifically valuable -- they sharpen the contribution
